element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Six stages to learn throttle control and turning on straightforward track design
Maintained throughout initial gameplay before turbo unlock
Enables higher-speed racing across later stages; optional skill ceiling expansion
Player agency in difficulty selection; Africa serves as tutorial equivalent
Each continent increases stage count and implied difficulty; Americas (14), Europe (18)
Money earned from races purchases bike component enhancements; meta-progression layer

typical_relief_triggers: Money earned in Original Mode (Genesis version)
typical_tension_triggers: Time pressure inherent to racing format
Increasing track difficulty from Africa → Europe
Higher opponent speeds on harder courses
Turbo mode engagement (risk/reward of higher speeds)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Constant forward momentum with progressive difficulty ramp; arcade pacing prioritizes continuous flow over stage-based relief cycles. Difficulty gates tied to course selection rather than scripted tension arcs.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Africa course (6 stages) serves as entry-level difficulty with straightforward track design and moderate obstacle density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Asia course (10 stages, matches original Hang-On length) and Americas course (14 stages) present escalating complexity; turbo mode becomes essential tool
difficulty_curve.late_game: Europe course (18 stages) represents peak difficulty with most challenging track layouts and opponent behavior
notable_spikes: Transition from Africa to Asia introduces stage count and complexity increase
Americas course introduces significant jump in stage count (14 vs 10)
Europe course final stages combine maximum stage count with maximum track difficulty

notable_level_patterns.description: Four distinct courses representing increasing difficulty through geographic framing. Africa (easiest, 6 stages) → Asia (moderate, 10 stages) → Americas (hard, 14 stages) → Europe (hardest, 18 stages). Each course is self-contained selectable unit allowing player-driven difficulty choice.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Africa course baseline vs Europe course endgame

notable_level_patterns.description: Genesis version stages are roughly half the length of original Hang-On stages, allowing for higher total stage count per course and faster progression rhythm
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Super Hang-On stage length vs original Hang-On equivalent

notable_level_patterns.description: Turbo mode (324 km/h) unlocks conditionally upon reaching 280 km/h base speed threshold, creating implicit skill gate and reward for consistent play
